question: Did external statebuilding and intervention in Syria enact Zaum’s sovereignty paradox?
rubric: Defines the paradox as external actors claiming to restore sovereignty while exercising trusteeship-like powers that hollow it.
Specifies which external projects (diplomatic track, reconstruction conditionality, security patronage, local governance support) count as statebuilding-like.
Distinguishes effects on Damascus’s juridical standing from effects on rival de-facto authorities’ autonomy.
Does not treat all foreign military presence as automatically 'statebuilding'.
Surfaces limits of applying post-conflict trusteeship cases to an active multi-patron war.
instant_dismissal_criteria: Equates any foreign troop presence with statebuilding
Assumes external aid always strengthens sovereignty
No engagement with Zaum’s norm/politics tension
Answers only with great-power scorekeeping and no sovereignty concept
notes: Hard because Syria is not a classic UN transitional administration case; analogy must be argued, not assumed.
